heading: Revocations of airman certificates for controlled substance violations

children.heading: .—Definition
children.label: a .—Definition
children.text: In this section, “controlled substance” has the same meaning given that term in section 102 of the Comprehensive Drug Abuse Prevention and Control Act of 1970 ().21 U.S.C. 802

children.label: A
children.text: an aircraft was used to commit, or facilitate the commission of, the offense; and

children.label: B
children.text: the individual served as an airman, or was on the aircraft, in connection with committing, or facilitating the commission of, the offense.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual knowingly carried out an activity punishable, under a law of the United States or a State related to a controlled substance (except a law related to simple possession of a controlled substance), by death or imprisonment for more than one year;

children.label: B
children.text: an aircraft was used to carry out or facilitate the activity; and

children.label: C
children.text: the individual served as an airman, or was on the aircraft, in connection with carrying out, or facilitating the carrying out of, the activity.
The Administrator has no authority under paragraph (1) of this subsection to review whether an airman violated a law of the United States or a State related to a controlled substance.

children.heading: .—Advice to Holders and Opportunity To Answer
children.label: c .—Advice to Holders and Opportunity To Answer

children.text: advise the holder of the certificate of the charges or reasons on which the Administrator relies for the proposed revocation; and
provide the holder of the certificate an opportunity to answer the charges and be heard why the certificate should not be revoked.

An individual whose certificate is revoked by the Administrator under subsection (b) of this section may appeal the revocation order to the National Transportation Safety Board. The Board shall affirm or reverse the order after providing notice and an opportunity for a hearing on the record. When conducting the hearing, the Board is not bound by findings of fact of the Administrator.

children.label: B
children.text: the Board shall make a final disposition of the appeal not later than 60 days after the Administrator so advises the Board.

An individual substantially affected by an order of the Board under this subsection, or the Administrator when the Administrator decides that an order of the Board will have a significant adverse effect on carrying out this part, may obtain judicial review of the order under . The Administrator shall be made a party to the judicial review proceedings. Findings of fact of the Board are conclusive if supported by substantial evidence.section 46110 of this title

The Administrator may not revoke, and the Board may not affirm a revocation of, an airman certificate under subsection (b)(2) of this section on the basis of an activity described in subsection (b)(2)(A) if the holder of the certificate is acquitted of all charges related to a controlled substance in an indictment or information arising from the activity.

children.label: A
children.text: the individual otherwise satisfies the requirements for a certificate under ; andsection 44703 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the individual subsequently is acquitted of all charges related to a controlled substance in an indictment or information arising from the activity; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the conviction on which a revocation under subsection (b)(1) of this section is based is reversed.
a law enforcement official of the United States Government or of a State requests a waiver; and
the Administrator decides that the waiver will facilitate law enforcement efforts.
